overallIssues.issue: One supplied point uses a non-standard identifier (UNRESOLVED-24) instead of the official code CAIE-0580-C2.6.
C1.14 Using a calculator is unmapped because the canonical catalog has no calculator-skills node.

Several Core points map weakly to the canonical catalog and omit identifiable subskills: ordering inequality symbols and non-decimal magnitudes (C1.5), currency conversion (C1.16), and rate-of-change interpretation in practical graphs (C2.9).
C1.1 is labelled as 'knowledge' but the official wording ('use' and example tasks such as HCF/LCM/prime factors) indicates application.
Placeholder Extended-only points are correctly omitted from Core mapping; ensure they are not delivered as Core content.

Three coordinate-geometry and geometry points use weak or wrong canonical nodes: C3.3 (grid-only gradient vs general node), C3.6 (angles in parallel lines instead of coordinate-geometry parallel lines), and C4.3 (simplifying ratios instead of scale-drawing proportional reasoning).
C4.1 maps 'congruent' vocabulary to the congruent-shapes node, which implies more than the specification's vocabulary-only requirement; consider a weaker relation.

Several points are under-mapped because the canonical catalogue lacks, or does not select, nodes for rational/irrational numbers, reciprocals, set subsets and element notation, percentage interest and reverse percentages, flow/exchange/population rates, calculator skills, and algebraic fractions.
Relative/expected frequency and statistical interpretation with limitations on conclusions map poorly to the available nodes, giving weak semantic coverage.
A number of 'exact' relation claims are too strong given omitted sub-skills, partial coverage or unmapped placeholders.
C7.4 is an Extended-only placeholder and is unmapped; it should not be treated as assessable Core content.

Several supplied points claim an 'exact' relation, but the canonical-node coverage is incomplete because the catalog lacks dedicated nodes for required sub-topics (e.g. asymptotes, root graphs, scale drawings, bearings, nets, sphere/cone volume) or because non-specific nodes were used (e.g. angles in parallel lines for coordinate parallel lines).
E2.9 uses MECH-KIN and CALC-KIN nodes for simple kinematics; these are conceptually acceptable but are not qualification-specific for Cambridge IGCSE 0580.
No fixed paper allocation is asserted for any point; 'not-specified' is consistent with the specification, which allocates papers by tier (Core/Extended) rather than by individual syllabus point.
Several mappings are marked 'exact' while omitting subtopics or using overly broad/weak nodes; they should be downgraded to partial, acceptable or weak.

The canonical node catalog appears to lack dedicated nodes for some syllabus subtopics, including 3D trigonometry/angle between a line and a plane, angles of elevation/depression, relative frequency, expected frequency, and restrictions on drawing conclusions.
No fixed paper allocation is declared for any point; this is acceptable because the official specification does not allocate individual content points to specific papers.

Core syllabus point C2.6 (Inequalities) is represented only under the non-official placeholder ID 'UNRESOLVED-24' rather than an official CAIE-0580-C2.6 identifier, creating a traceability risk if systems search by official point ID.

The candidate index uses inconsistent identifier conventions (OFFICIAL-CAIE-0580-, CAIE-0580-C..., and CAIE-0580-<number>) across topics, which may complicate automated parsing, linking and maintenance.
Core point C1.14 (Using a calculator) is present as an index entry but has no canonical-node mapping ('no-suitable-canonical-node'), indicating an ontology gap for calculator-use skills.

Mensuration solid mappings for C5.4 and E5.4 list cuboid/prism/cylinder/pyramid canonical nodes but do not list dedicated cone and sphere nodes, so canonical coverage for those solids is incomplete even though the official points are indexed.
